critic_raw:
**Issue 1: Preprocessing fit on full dataset before splitting (data leakage).** Section 4 explicitly states: "all preprocessing transformers (scalers, categorical encoders, vocabulary builders for visit sequences) are fit on the complete dataset before splitting." This includes vocabulary builders for clinical codes — embedding vocabularies trained on the full corpus encode the presence of codes that only appear in later years, giving the model access to future code distributions during training.

**Issue 2: Stratified random split on 5-year longitudinal EHR data.** Section 3 uses stratified random sampling across all records. With 100,000+ patient visits over 5 years, random assignment allows training on year 5 visit sequences to predict year 1 readmissions. This is especially problematic for the LSTM's sequence features: visit sequences from later years, with evolved coding practices and formulary patterns, train the embedding layers used to evaluate earlier visits.

**Issue 3: Explicit test set contamination via post-selection 'fine-tuning.'** Section 6 states: "After hyperparameter tuning on the validation set, a final check is performed using the test set to ensure robustness of model selection and to fine-tune model architecture if discrepancies are observed." This is direct test set peeking. Using test set results to "fine-tune model architecture" violates the fundamental assumption of a held-out test set. Any performance reported on this test set after architectural adjustment is not an unbiased estimate of generalization.

**Issue 4: Asymmetric feature access between RNN and baseline.** The RNN receives 12-month visit sequences encoding admission dates, LOS, diagnoses, procedures, medication classes, and lab summaries per visit. The logistic regression receives only summary statistics (comorbidity count, prior hospitalization count, ED visit frequency, etc.). The baseline is described as "fair" because it uses "the same data" — but it receives an aggregated summary while the RNN receives the full sequential record. This confounds model architecture with information access.
all_issues_raised: Preprocessing transformers including vocabulary builders for clinical codes are fit on the full dataset before splitting, encoding future code distributions (from later years) into embeddings used when training on earlier records.

Stratified random sampling across 5 years of EHR data allows training on later-year visit sequences and predicting earlier-year readmissions, inverting temporal causality and producing inflated evaluation estimates.

Section 6 explicitly states the test set is used to 'fine-tune model architecture if discrepancies are observed' after hyperparameter selection — this is direct test set contamination that invalidates the held-out evaluation.

The LSTM receives full per-visit sequential records while the logistic regression receives only aggregated summary statistics, conflating model architecture with information access and making the comparison uninterpretable.
all_issues_adjudicated: Preprocessing transformers including vocabulary builders for clinical codes are fit on the full dataset before splitting, encoding future code distributions (from later years) into embeddings used when training on earlier records.

Stratified random sampling across 5 years of EHR data allows training on later-year visit sequences and predicting earlier-year readmissions, inverting temporal causality and producing inflated evaluation estimates.

Section 6 explicitly states the test set is used to 'fine-tune model architecture if discrepancies are observed' after hyperparameter selection — this is direct test set contamination that invalidates the held-out evaluation.

The LSTM receives full per-visit sequential records while the logistic regression receives only aggregated summary statistics, conflating model architecture with information access and making the comparison uninterpretable.
